title: Muscular dystrophy - NHS
text:
as a pacemaker or medicines like ACE inhibitors and beta blockers surgery for problems like scoliosis and tight joints a machine to help with your breathing, if the condition affects your lungs Support for living with muscular dystrophy You'll also be offered support for living with muscular dystrophy. Every person's needs will be different depending on their symptoms.
Support you might need may include:
occupational therapy to help you do day-to-day activities mobility equipment , including walking sticks, frames and wheelchairs home adaptations , such as stair lifts or railings support around the home, at work and school 
Help and support for muscular dystrophy 
If you or your child have muscular dystrophy, your care team will be there to support you throughout your treatment.
You may also find it helpful to get support from other people with muscular dystrophy, or parents of children with muscular dystrophy.
There are also national charities that can offer help and support.
Muscular Dystrophy UK Muscular Dystrophy UK is a charity that provides support and information to people with muscular dystrophy and other muscle wasting conditions, and their family and carers.
